title: $split
text: $split Split a string. Return = $split ( Source , StartPos , SearchFor { , LeftPart { , RightPart }} ) Example: vStatus = $split(vFieldname, 1, ".",vField, vEntity) Parameters Table 1. Parameters Parameter Data Type Direction Description Source String In String that needs to be split. StartPos Numeric In Position in Source where the search needs to start. SearchFor String In Substring to find; can be a constant string, regex string, or syntax string. LeftPart String Out String to the left of the split. RightPart String Out String to the right of the split. Use Allowed in all component types. Return Values Position of the SearchFor substring within the string. If the SearchFor substring is not found, $split returns 0 in $status and the Out parameters LeftPart and RightPart are empty. Example: $split In the following example, a field name that includes the entity name is split into the field and entity parts by the dot. variables string vFieldname string vField, vEntity numeric vStatus endVariables vFieldname = "Field.Entity" vStatus = $split(vFieldname, 1, ".",vField, vEntity) ; vStatus = 6 ; vField = "Field" ; vEntity = "entity" Copy In the following example, a string is split into parts by the colon, dot, or semicolon. Explain this code variables string vText, vPre numeric vStatus endVariables vStatus = -1 vText = "Uniface:Field.Entity;Good" while(vStatus != 0) vStatus = $split(vText,1,$regex("[:|.|;]"),vPre, vText) endwhile ; First loop: vPre is "Uniface", vText is "Field.Entity;Good", vStatus is 8. ; Second loop: vPre is " Field ", vText is "Entity;Good", vStatus is 6. ; Third loop: vPre is " Entity ", vText is "Good", vStatus is 7. ; Fourth loop: vPre is NULL, vText is NULL, vStatus is 0.
